---
title: 'Wind Compensation in Ski Jumping: Model Validation Women'
author:
- Dr. Gavin Taylor
- MIDN Toren Hawk
- MIDN Travis Hockin
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
fontsize: 11pt
geometry: margin=1in
---

Please note, many ggplot and ggsaves have been commented out to avoid saving unwanted graphs to your computer. To save them, simply uncomment ggplots and saves.

```{r setup-theme, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Packages (hidden for cleanliness)
library(tidyverse)
library(caret)
library(randomForest)
library(tibble)
library(ggplot2)
library(mgcv)
library(dplyr)

# ============================================================
# Global Theme + Palette for All Ski Jumping Plots
# (Persuading with Data–style, no margin() dependency)
# ============================================================

# Model / wind palettes --------------------------------------

ski_palette <- c(
  "Linear"       = "#0072B2",  # Blue
  "RandomForest" = "#D55E00",  # Vermillion (orange-red)
  "Polynomial"   = "#009E73",  # Bluish green
  "GAM"          = "#CC79A7",  # Reddish purple
  "head"         = "#0072B2",  # Blue
  "tail"         = "#D55E00"   # Vermillion
)


poster_palette <- c(
  "head"         = "#56B4E9",  # Sky blue
  "tail"         = "#009E73",  # Bluish green
  "Linear"       = "#56B4E9",  # Sky blue
  "RandomForest" = "#009E73",  # Bluish green
  "Polynomial"   = "#E69F00",  # Yellow-orange
  "GAM"          = "#CC79A7"   # Reddish purple
)


# Core theme -------------------------------------------------

theme_ski <- function(base_size = 14,
                      base_family = "sans",
                      legend_pos = "bottom") {

  theme_minimal(base_size = base_size, base_family = base_family) +
    theme(
      # --- Titles / subtitles / caption ---
      plot.title = element_text(
        face = "bold",
        size = base_size + 2,
        hjust = 0   # left aligned
      ),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(
        size = base_size,
        hjust = 0
      ),
      plot.caption = element_text(
        size  = base_size - 2,
        hjust = 1,
        colour = "grey40"
      ),

      # --- Axes ---
      axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
      axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold"),
      axis.text    = element_text(colour = "grey20"),

      # --- Gridlines: horizontal only ---
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.y = element_line(
        colour    = "grey90",
        linewidth = 0.3
      ),

      # --- Backgrounds set to white ---
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
      plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
      legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),

      # --- Axis & panel lines ---
      panel.border = element_blank(),
      axis.line    = element_line(colour = "black"),

      # --- Legend ---
      legend.position = legend_pos,
      legend.title    = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.key      = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),

      # --- Facet strips (now white instead of grey) ---
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
      strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold")
    )
}

  

# Color / fill scales ----------------------------------------

scale_color_ski <- function(..., palette = c("paper", "poster")) {
  palette <- match.arg(palette)
  vals <- if (palette == "paper") ski_palette else poster_palette
  scale_color_manual(values = vals, ...)
}

scale_fill_ski <- function(..., palette = c("paper", "poster")) {
  palette <- match.arg(palette)
  vals <- if (palette == "paper") ski_palette else poster_palette
  scale_fill_manual(values = vals, ...)
}

# Set default theme for all plots in this session
theme_set(theme_ski())

exists("scale_color_ski")
exists("scale_fill_ski")
exists("theme_ski")

```



```{r}
# Read dataset and apply core filters
df <- read_csv("../data/female_WC_21-25.csv") %>%
  filter(!is.na(Distance_m))

df_clean <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(Rank), Rank <= 20) %>%
  filter(!is.na(WindMS), WindMS >= -4, WindMS <= 4) %>%
  mutate(Location = if_else(Location == "PERTILE Sandro", "Lake Placid", Location))

glimpse(df_clean)


```


```{r}
# Baseline linear model on core predictors
predictors <- c("Speed_kmh", "WindMS", "HillSize_m")
predictor_df <- df_clean %>% select(Distance_m, all_of(predictors)) %>% drop_na()
linear_model_importance <- lm(Distance_m ~ Speed_kmh + WindMS + HillSize_m, data = predictor_df)

summary(linear_model_importance)
glimpse(predictor_df)

```


```{r}
# Purpose: Construct hill-size bins and attach them to the modeling table.
# Bins: Small (≤49), Medium (50–84), Normal (85–109), Large (110–184), Ski Flying (≥185).
# Why: Wind and speed effects vary by hill size. Stratification supports fair comparisons.
# Output: hill data frame with HillBin factor and no missing values in key fields.
hill_bin <- function(HillSize_m) dplyr::case_when(
  !is.na(HillSize_m) & HillSize_m <= 49   ~ "Small (≤49)",
  !is.na(HillSize_m) & HillSize_m <= 84   ~ "Medium (50–84)",
  !is.na(HillSize_m) & HillSize_m <= 109  ~ "Normal (85–109)",
  !is.na(HillSize_m) & HillSize_m <= 184  ~ "Large (110–184)",
  !is.na(HillSize_m) & HillSize_m >= 185  ~ "Ski Flying (≥185)",
  TRUE ~ NA_character_
)

hill <- predictor_df %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    HillSize_m = as.numeric(HillSize_m),
    HillBin = hill_bin(HillSize_m),
    HillBin = factor(HillBin, levels = c(
      "Small (≤49)","Medium (50–84)","Normal (85–109)",
      "Large (110–184)","Ski Flying (≥185)"
    ))
  ) %>%
  drop_na(Distance_m, Speed_kmh, WindMS, HillSize_m, HillBin)
  
hill %>%
count(HillBin, name = "n_jumps") %>%
arrange(factor(HillBin, levels = c(
"Small (≤49)","Medium (50–84)","Normal (85–109)",
"Large (110–184)","Ski Flying (≥185)"
)))

hill_counts <- hill %>%
  dplyr::count(HillBin, name = "n_jumps")

# Classify wind direction and count per hill bin + wind side
hill_counts_side <- hill %>%
  mutate(
    WindSide = case_when(
      WindMS > 0  ~ "head",
      WindMS < 0  ~ "tail",
      TRUE        ~ NA_character_   # treat WindMS == 0 as missing for this plot
    )
  ) %>%
  drop_na(WindSide) %>%
  count(HillBin, WindSide, name = "n_jumps")

hill_counts_side

# Plot head vs tail counts per hill bin
p <- ggplot(hill_counts_side,
            aes(x = HillBin, y = n_jumps, fill = WindSide)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = scales::comma(n_jumps)),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
    vjust = -0.3,
    size = 3.8
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = ski_palette[c("head", "tail")],
    labels = c(head = "Headwind", tail = "Tailwind"),
    name   = "Wind direction"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Most female athlete jumps occur on normal and large hills",
    subtitle = "Number of headwind and tailwind jumps by hill size category",
    x        = "Hill size category",
    y        = ""
  ) +
  theme_ski() +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off")

p

#ggsave("female/hill_bin_counts.png", plot = p, width = 16, height = 12, dpi = 600, type = "cairo-png")



```



```{r}
# Purpose: Define evaluation metrics used across models.
# Metrics: RMSE (scale of meters), R2 (variance explained on the test split).
# --- metrics once ---
rmse <- function(y, yhat) sqrt(mean((y - yhat)^2))
r2   <- function(y, yhat) 1 - sum((y - yhat)^2) / sum((y - mean(y))^2)
# Helper: Mean Absolute Percentage Error (in percent)
mape <- function(actual, predicted) {
  mean(abs((actual - predicted) / actual)) * 100
}

```


```{r}
# Purpose: Train four models with WindMS included and score on a held-out test set (80/20 split).
# Models: Linear, degree-2 Polynomial, GAM (thin-plate splines), Random Forest.
# Details: Adaptive k for GAM avoids overfitting/underidentification on sparse grids.
# Output: Tibble of RMSE, R2, and MAPE sorted by RMSE for the provided hill-bin subset.
fit_and_score_wind <- function(df_bin, seed = 2026) {
  df_bin <- df_bin %>% drop_na(Distance_m, Speed_kmh, WindMS, HillSize_m)
  if (nrow(df_bin) < 20) {
    return(tibble(
      Model = c("Linear","Polynomial","GAM","RandomForest"),
      RMSE  = NA_real_,
      R2    = NA_real_,
      MAPE  = NA_real_
    ))
  }
  set.seed(seed)
  n   <- nrow(df_bin)
  idx <- sample(seq_len(n), size = floor(0.8 * n))
  train <- df_bin[idx, ]
  test  <- df_bin[-idx, ]

  k_speed <- max(3, min(6, length(unique(train$Speed_kmh)) - 1))
  k_wind  <- max(3, min(6, length(unique(train$WindMS)) - 1))

  m_lm   <- lm(Distance_m ~ Speed_kmh + WindMS + HillSize_m, data = train)
  m_poly <- lm(Distance_m ~ poly(Speed_kmh, 2, raw = TRUE) +
                               poly(WindMS,   2, raw = TRUE) +
                               HillSize_m, data = train)
  m_gam  <- mgcv::gam(Distance_m ~ s(Speed_kmh, k = k_speed) +
                                   s(WindMS,   k = k_wind)  +
                                   HillSize_m,
                      data = train, method = "REML", select = TRUE)
  m_rf   <- randomForest::randomForest(
              Distance_m ~ Speed_kmh + WindMS + HillSize_m,
              data = train, importance = TRUE
            )

  preds <- list(
    Linear       = predict(m_lm,   test),
    Polynomial   = predict(m_poly, test),
    GAM          = predict(m_gam,  test),
    RandomForest = predict(m_rf,   test)
  )

  tibble(
    Model = names(preds),
    RMSE  = purrr::map_dbl(preds, ~ rmse(test$Distance_m, .x)),
    R2    = purrr::map_dbl(preds, ~ r2(test$Distance_m, .x)),
    MAPE  = purrr::map_dbl(preds, ~ mape(test$Distance_m, .x))
  ) %>% arrange(RMSE)
}

```


```{r}
# Purpose: Same as above but exclude WindMS to quantify its incremental value.
# Models and split: Identical choices and 80/20 partition as with-wind pipeline.
# Output: Tibble of RMSE, R2, and MAPE for the no-wind specification.
fit_and_score_nowind <- function(df_bin, seed = 2026) {
  df_bin <- df_bin %>% drop_na(Distance_m, Speed_kmh, HillSize_m)
  if (nrow(df_bin) < 20) {
    return(tibble(
      Model = c("Linear","Polynomial","GAM","RandomForest"),
      RMSE  = NA_real_,
      R2    = NA_real_,
      MAPE  = NA_real_
    ))
  }
  set.seed(seed)
  n   <- nrow(df_bin)
  idx <- sample(seq_len(n), size = floor(0.8 * n))
  train <- df_bin[idx, ]
  test  <- df_bin[-idx, ]

  k_speed <- max(3, min(6, length(unique(train$Speed_kmh)) - 1))

  m_lm   <- lm(Distance_m ~ Speed_kmh + HillSize_m, data = train)
  m_poly <- lm(Distance_m ~ poly(Speed_kmh, 2, raw = TRUE) + HillSize_m, data = train)
  m_gam  <- mgcv::gam(Distance_m ~ s(Speed_kmh, k = k_speed) + HillSize_m,
                      data = train, method = "REML", select = TRUE)
  m_rf   <- randomForest::randomForest(
              Distance_m ~ Speed_kmh + HillSize_m,
              data = train, importance = TRUE
            )

  preds <- list(
    Linear       = predict(m_lm,   test),
    Polynomial   = predict(m_poly, test),
    GAM          = predict(m_gam,  test),
    RandomForest = predict(m_rf,   test)
  )

  tibble(
    Model = names(preds),
    RMSE  = purrr::map_dbl(preds, ~ rmse(test$Distance_m, .x)),
    R2    = purrr::map_dbl(preds, ~ r2(test$Distance_m, .x)),
    MAPE  = purrr::map_dbl(preds, ~ mape(test$Distance_m, .x))
  ) %>% arrange(RMSE)
}

```


```{r}
# Purpose: Train four models with WindMS included and score on a held-out test set (80/20 split).
# Models: Linear, degree-2 Polynomial, GAM (thin-plate splines), Random Forest.
# Details: Adaptive k for GAM avoids overfitting/underidentification on sparse grids.
# Output: Tibble of RMSE and R2 sorted by RMSE for the provided hill-bin subset.
res_small_wind  <- hill %>% filter(HillBin == "Small (≤49)")       %>% fit_and_score_wind()
res_med_wind    <- hill %>% filter(HillBin == "Medium (50–84)")     %>% fit_and_score_wind()
res_norm_wind   <- hill %>% filter(HillBin == "Normal (85–109)")    %>% fit_and_score_wind()
res_large_wind  <- hill %>% filter(HillBin == "Large (110–184)")    %>% fit_and_score_wind()
res_sf_wind     <- hill %>% filter(HillBin == "Ski Flying (≥185)")  %>% fit_and_score_wind()

res_norm_wind
res_large_wind
res_sf_wind
```



```{r}
# Purpose: Same as above but exclude WindMS to quantify its incremental value.
# Models and split: Identical choices and 80/20 partition as with-wind pipeline.
# Output: Tibble of RMSE and R2 for the no-wind specification.
res_small_now   <- hill %>% filter(HillBin == "Small (≤49)")       %>% fit_and_score_nowind()
res_med_now     <- hill %>% filter(HillBin == "Medium (50–84)")     %>% fit_and_score_nowind()
res_norm_now    <- hill %>% filter(HillBin == "Normal (85–109)")    %>% fit_and_score_nowind()
res_large_now   <- hill %>% filter(HillBin == "Large (110–184)")    %>% fit_and_score_nowind()
res_sf_now      <- hill %>% filter(HillBin == "Ski Flying (≥185)")  %>% fit_and_score_nowind()

res_norm_now
res_large_now
res_sf_now
```


```{r}
# Purpose: Function to visualize predicted vs. actual distances for four model families.
# Notes: Uses an 80/20 split. Facets by model. 45-degree line indicates perfect fit.
# Option: with_wind switch toggles inclusion of WindMS in the models.
plot_pred_vs_actual <- function(df_bin, with_wind = TRUE, seed = 2026, title_suffix = "") {
  if (with_wind) {
    df_bin <- df_bin %>% drop_na(Distance_m, Speed_kmh, WindMS, HillSize_m)
  } else {
    df_bin <- df_bin %>% drop_na(Distance_m, Speed_kmh, HillSize_m)
  }
  if (nrow(df_bin) < 20) {
    warning("Not enough rows to plot.")
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }
  set.seed(seed)
  n   <- nrow(df_bin)
  idx <- sample(seq_len(n), size = floor(0.8 * n))
  train <- df_bin[idx, ]
  test  <- df_bin[-idx, ]

  if (with_wind) {
    k_speed <- max(3, min(6, length(unique(train$Speed_kmh)) - 1))
    k_wind  <- max(3, min(6, length(unique(train$WindMS)) - 1))

    m_lm   <- lm(Distance_m ~ Speed_kmh + WindMS + HillSize_m, data = train)
    m_poly <- lm(Distance_m ~ poly(Speed_kmh, 2, raw = TRUE) +
                                 poly(WindMS,   2, raw = TRUE) +
                                 HillSize_m, data = train)
    m_gam  <- mgcv::gam(Distance_m ~ s(Speed_kmh, k = k_speed) +
                                     s(WindMS,   k = k_wind)  +
                                     HillSize_m,
                        data = train, method = "REML", select = TRUE)
    m_rf   <- randomForest::randomForest(
                Distance_m ~ Speed_kmh + WindMS + HillSize_m,
                data = train, importance = TRUE
              )
  } else {
    k_speed <- max(3, min(6, length(unique(train$Speed_kmh)) - 1))
    m_lm   <- lm(Distance_m ~ Speed_kmh + HillSize_m, data = train)
    m_poly <- lm(Distance_m ~ poly(Speed_kmh, 2, raw = TRUE) + HillSize_m, data = train)
    m_gam  <- mgcv::gam(Distance_m ~ s(Speed_kmh, k = k_speed) + HillSize_m,
                        data = train, method = "REML", select = TRUE)
    m_rf   <- randomForest::randomForest(
                Distance_m ~ Speed_kmh + HillSize_m,
                data = train, importance = TRUE
              )
  }

  preds <- tibble(
    Linear       = predict(m_lm,   test),
    Polynomial   = predict(m_poly, test),
    GAM          = predict(m_gam,  test),
    RandomForest = predict(m_rf,   test)
  )

  long <- preds %>%
    mutate(Actual = test$Distance_m) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = c(Linear, Polynomial, GAM, RandomForest),
                 names_to = "Model", values_to = "Predicted")

  ggplot(long, aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted, color = Model)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.35) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~ Model, scales = "free") +
  labs(
    title = paste0("Predicted vs Actual Distance (Female Athletes)\n", title_suffix),
    subtitle = if (with_wind) "Models include WindMS" else "Models exclude WindMS",
    x = "Actual Distance (m)",
    y = "Predicted Distance (m)"
  ) + scale_fill_ski() 


}


# ======================================================
# 2) Estimated Wind Effect — all models in one chart
#    - Colored lines + legend
#    - Zero reference line at WindMS = 0
#    - Clear axis labelling (+ headwind, – tailwind)
# ======================================================
plot_wind_effect <- function(df_bin, wind_range = NULL, seed = 2026, title_suffix = "") {

  df_bin <- df_bin %>% drop_na(Distance_m, Speed_kmh, WindMS, HillSize_m)
  if (nrow(df_bin) < 20) {
    warning("Not enough rows to plot.")
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  set.seed(seed)
  n   <- nrow(df_bin)
  idx <- sample(seq_len(n), size = floor(0.8 * n))
  train <- df_bin[idx, ]

  k_speed <- max(3, min(6, length(unique(train$Speed_kmh)) - 1))
  k_wind  <- max(3, min(6, length(unique(train$WindMS)) - 1))

  m_lm   <- lm(Distance_m ~ Speed_kmh + WindMS + HillSize_m, data = train)
  m_poly <- lm(Distance_m ~ poly(Speed_kmh, 2, raw = TRUE) +
                               poly(WindMS,   2, raw = TRUE) +
                               HillSize_m, data = train)
  m_gam  <- mgcv::gam(Distance_m ~ s(Speed_kmh, k = k_speed) +
                                   s(WindMS,   k = k_wind)  +
                                   HillSize_m,
                      data = train, method = "REML", select = TRUE)
  m_rf   <- randomForest::randomForest(
    Distance_m ~ Speed_kmh + WindMS + HillSize_m,
    data = train, importance = TRUE
  )

  med_speed <- median(train$Speed_kmh, na.rm = TRUE)
  med_hs    <- median(train$HillSize_m, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (is.null(wind_range)) {
    wmin <- quantile(train$WindMS, 0.02, na.rm = TRUE)
    wmax <- quantile(train$WindMS, 0.98, na.rm = TRUE)
  } else {
    wmin <- wind_range[1]; wmax <- wind_range[2]
  }

  newd <- tibble(
    Speed_kmh  = med_speed,
    WindMS     = seq(wmin, wmax, length.out = 200),
    HillSize_m = med_hs
  )

  out <- tibble(
    WindMS        = newd$WindMS,
    Linear        = predict(m_lm,   newd),
    Polynomial    = predict(m_poly, newd),
    GAM           = predict(m_gam,  newd),
    RandomForest  = predict(m_rf,   newd)
  ) %>%
    pivot_longer(-WindMS, names_to = "Model", values_to = "Predicted_Distance")

    ggplot(out, aes(WindMS, Predicted_Distance, 
                color = Model, 
                linetype = Model)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 3) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c(
    "Linear"       = "solid",
    "Polynomial"   = "dashed",
    "GAM"          = "dotdash",
    "RandomForest" = "twodash"
  )) +
  scale_color_ski() +
  labs(
    title = paste0("Estimated Wind Effect on Distance (Female Athletes)", title_suffix),
    subtitle = "Speed_kmh and HillSize_m held at medians",
    x = "WindMS (+ headwind / − tailwind)",
    y = "Predicted Distance (m)"
  ) +
  theme_ski()

}
```





```{r}
# Purpose: Generate core figures for representative bins (Normal, Large, Ski Flying).
# Plots: (1) Predicted vs Actual, (2) Estimated Wind Effect curves.
# Option: An extra call illustrates models without WindMS for sensitivity.


# Normal hill (with wind)
hill_norm <- hill %>% filter(HillBin == "Normal (85–109)")

p <- plot_pred_vs_actual(hill_norm, with_wind = TRUE,  title_suffix = "— Normal (85–109)") + scale_color_ski()
#ggsave("female/norm_pred_v_act.png", plot = p, width = 16, height = 12, dpi = 600, type = "cairo-png")
p <- plot_wind_effect(hill_norm, title_suffix = "— Normal (85–109)") + scale_color_ski()
#ggsave("female/norm_wind_effect.png", plot = p, width = 16, height = 12, dpi = 600, type = "cairo-png")

# Large hill (with and without wind)
hill_large <- hill %>% filter(HillBin == "Large (110–184)")
p <- plot_pred_vs_actual(hill_large, with_wind = TRUE,  title_suffix = "— Large (110–184)") + scale_color_ski()
#ggsave("female/large_pred_v_act.png", plot = p, width = 16, height = 12, dpi = 600, type = "cairo-png")
p <- plot_wind_effect(hill_large,                        title_suffix = "— Large (110–184)") + scale_color_ski()
#ggsave("female/large_wind_effect.png", plot = p, width = 16, height = 12, dpi = 600, type = "cairo-png")
p <- plot_pred_vs_actual(hill_large, with_wind = FALSE, title_suffix = "— Large (110–184), No-Wind") + scale_color_ski()
#ggsave("female/large_pred_v_act_nowind.png", plot = p, width = 16, height = 12, dpi = 600, type = "cairo-png")

# Ski flying
hill_sf <- hill %>% filter(HillBin == "Ski Flying (≥185)")
p <- plot_pred_vs_actual(hill_sf, with_wind = TRUE, title_suffix = "— Ski Flying (≥185)") + scale_color_ski()
#ggsave("female/sf_pred_v_act.png", plot = p, width = 16, height = 12, dpi = 600, type = "cairo-png")
p <- plot_wind_effect(hill_sf, title_suffix = "— Ski Flying (≥185)") + scale_color_ski()
#ggsave("female/sf_wind_effect.png", plot = p, width = 16, height = 12, dpi = 600, type = "cairo-png")

```


```{r}
# Purpose: Descriptive count of jump observations by Location and HillSize_m.
# Why: Confirms sample sizes driving model stability and highlights data imbalance.

df_clean %>%
  mutate(
    WindSide = case_when(
      WindMS > 0  ~ "head",
      WindMS < 0  ~ "tail",
      TRUE        ~ "calm"   # keep zero-wind if you want to track it; remove if not needed
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(Location, HillSize_m) %>%
  summarise(
    total_jumps = n(),
    head_wind_jumps = sum(WindSide == "head", na.rm = TRUE),
    tail_wind_jumps = sum(WindSide == "tail", na.rm = TRUE),
    # remove calm if unused — or uncomment to show:
    # calm_jumps = sum(WindSide == "calm", na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_jumps))
```


```{r}
# Purpose: Case study at Hinzenbach (Normal hill, HS 85–109).
# Method: Fit Random Forest and Linear models on the same 80/20 split; compare predictions
#         and visualize actual vs predicted scatter with 1:1 reference.
# Output: Head of error tables and two diagnostic plots.
fit_distance_models_by_location <- function(df_clean,
                                            location,
                                            hs_min = 85,
                                            hs_max = 109,
                                            seed   = 2026,
                                            rf_ntree = 500) {
  location = as.character(location)
  dat_loc <- df_clean %>%
    filter(Location == location,
           HillSize_m >= hs_min, HillSize_m <= hs_max) %>%
    drop_na(Distance_m, Speed_kmh, WindMS, HillSize_m)

  n <- nrow(dat_loc)
  if (is.na(n) || n < 10) {
    stop(glue::glue("Not enough rows for {location} (HS {hs_min}-{hs_max}). Found n = {n}."))
  }

  set.seed(seed)
  train_idx <- sample(seq_len(n), size = floor(0.8 * n))
  train <- dat_loc[train_idx, ]
  test  <- dat_loc[-train_idx, ]

  rf <- randomForest(
    Distance_m ~ Speed_kmh + WindMS + HillSize_m,
    data = train, importance = TRUE, ntree = rf_ntree
  )

  lm_fit <- lm(Distance_m ~ Speed_kmh + WindMS + HillSize_m, data = train)

  test_lm <- test %>%
    mutate(
      Predicted      = predict(lm_fit, newdata = .),
      Error          = Predicted - Distance_m,
      PercentError   = if_else(Distance_m != 0,
                               100 * (Predicted - Distance_m) / Distance_m,
                               NA_real_),
      AbsPercentError = abs(PercentError)
    )

  test_rf <- test %>%
    mutate(
      Predicted      = predict(rf, newdata = .),
      Error          = Predicted - Distance_m,
      PercentError   = if_else(Distance_m != 0,
                               100 * (Predicted - Distance_m) / Distance_m,
                               NA_real_),
      AbsPercentError = abs(PercentError)
    )

  rmse <- function(y, yhat) sqrt(mean((yhat - y)^2, na.rm = TRUE))

  summary_tbl <- tibble(
    Location = location,
    HS_Band  = glue::glue("{hs_min}-{hs_max}"),
    Model = c("Random Forest", "Linear Model"),
    MeanPercentError = c(mean(test_rf$PercentError,  na.rm = TRUE),
                         mean(test_lm$PercentError,  na.rm = TRUE)),
    MeanAbsPercentError = c(mean(test_rf$AbsPercentError, na.rm = TRUE),
                            mean(test_lm$AbsPercentError, na.rm = TRUE)),
    RMSE = c(rmse(test_rf$Distance_m, test_rf$Predicted),
             rmse(test_lm$Distance_m, test_lm$Predicted))
  )

  title_suffix <- glue::glue("{location} (HS {hs_min}-{hs_max})")

  p_rf <- ggplot(test_rf, aes(x = Distance_m, y = Predicted)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = "RandomForest"), alpha = 0.5, size = 2) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = glue::glue("Actual vs. predicted jump distance for Random Forest (80/20 test split) — {title_suffix}"),  # HEADLINE
    x = "Actual Distance (m)",
    y = "Predicted Distance (m)",
    color = "Model"
  ) +
  scale_color_ski() +
  theme_ski() +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 0, vjust = 0.5)   # HORIZONTAL Y-AXIS LABEL
  )


  p_lm <- ggplot(test_lm, aes(x = Distance_m, y = Predicted)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = "Linear"), alpha = 0.5, size =2) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = glue::glue("Actual vs. predicted jump distance for Linear Model (80/20 test split) — {title_suffix}"),  # HEADLINE
    x = "Actual Distance (m)",
    y = "Predicted Distance (m)",
    color = "Model"
  ) +
  scale_color_ski() +
  theme_ski() +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 0, vjust = 0.5)   # HORIZONTAL Y-AXIS LABEL
  )


# Combine both model results for faceting
plot_dat <- bind_rows(
  test_rf  %>% mutate(Model = "RandomForest"),
  test_lm  %>% mutate(Model = "Linear")
)

p_faceted <- ggplot(plot_dat, aes(x = Distance_m, y = Predicted, color = Model)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 2) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  facet_wrap(~ Model) +
  labs(
    title = glue::glue("Random Forest and Linear models both yield similar accuracy and results. — {title_suffix}"),   # HEADLINE
    subtitle = "Actual vs. predicted jump distances for Random Forest and Linear models (80/20 test split)", # WHAT THE GRAPH SHOWS
    x = "Actual Distance (m)",
    y = "Predicted Distance (m)",
    color = "Model"
  ) +
  scale_color_ski() +
  theme_ski() +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 0, vjust = 0.5)  # horizontal label
  )

  list(
    data_used     = dat_loc,
    train         = train,
    test_rf       = test_rf,
    test_lm       = test_lm,
    rf_model      = rf,
    lm_model      = lm_fit,
    performance   = summary_tbl,
    plot_rf       = p_rf,
    plot_lm       = p_lm,
    plot_faceted = p_faceted
  )
}
```



```{r}
# Example venue runs (large hill band)
res_trd <- fit_distance_models_by_location(df_clean, "Hinzenbach", 85, 109)

# Peek at predictions
head(res_trd$test_rf %>% select(Name, Distance_m, Predicted, Error, PercentError, AbsPercentError))
head(res_trd$test_lm %>% select(Name, Distance_m, Predicted, Error, PercentError, AbsPercentError))

# Compare models
res_trd$performance

# Plots

p = res_trd$plot_faceted
#ggsave("female/faceted_case_study.png", plot = p, width = 16, height = 12, dpi = 600, type = "cairo-png")
p <- res_trd$plot_rf
#ggsave("female/randfor_case_study.png", plot = p, width = 16, height = 12, dpi = 600, type = "cairo-png")
p <- res_trd$plot_lm
#ggsave("female/linear_case_study.png", plot = p, width = 16, height = 12, dpi = 600, type = "cairo-png")
```



```{r}
Lillehammer <- fit_distance_models_by_location(df_clean, "Lillehammer", 110, 184)
Sapporo <- fit_distance_models_by_location(df_clean, "Sapporo", 110, 184)
Oslo <- fit_distance_models_by_location(df_clean, "Oslo", 110, 184)
Willingen <- fit_distance_models_by_location(df_clean, "Willingen", 110, 184)
Lahti <- fit_distance_models_by_location(df_clean, "Lahti", 110, 184)

Lillehammer$performance
Sapporo$performance
Oslo$performance
Willingen$performance
Lahti$performance
```



```{r}
# ============================================================
# Wind Compensation Validation with 80/20 Train/Test Split
# Models compared: Linear vs Random Forest
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# What this does:
# - For each group (HillBin or Location) and wind side (head/tail),
#   split data 80/20 into train/test.
# - Fit two models on TRAIN:
#       (1) Linear: Distance_m ~ Speed_kmh + HillSize_m + WindMS
#       (2) Random Forest: same predictors
# - Derive a model-based wind factor (meters per m/s) for each model:
#       - Linear: the WindMS coefficient directly.
#       - RF: finite-difference derivative at a representative wind (w0)
# - Convert meters-per-(m/s) -> points-per-(m/s) using median MeterValue.
# - Evaluate on TEST: RMSE, R2, and residuals vs awarded WindPts.
# - Compare model-based factors to official HeadWindFactor/TailWindFactor.
# ============================================================

# PATCH to fix: "missing values in object" (na.fail in lm/predict)
# Cause: some groups/sides had NAs in required predictors/columns.
# Fix: aggressively drop NAs BEFORE splitting and again inside the side estimator,
#      and only score on complete test rows.

# -----------------------------
# 0) Utility: 80/20 split
# -----------------------------


df_clean <- df_clean %>%
  mutate(
    HillBin = hill_bin(HillSize_m),
    HillBin = factor(HillBin, levels = c(
      "Small (≤49)","Medium (50–84)","Normal (85–109)",
      "Large (110–184)","Ski Flying (≥185)"
    ))
  )


train_test_split <- function(df, prop = 0.8, seed = 2026) {
  set.seed(seed)
  n <- nrow(df)
  if (n < 10) return(list(train = df[0, ], test = df[0, ]))
  idx <- sample(seq_len(n), size = floor(prop * n))
  list(train = df[idx, ], test = df[-idx, ])
}

# ------------------------------------------------
# 1) RF local slope wrt WindMS via finite diff
# ------------------------------------------------
rf_wind_slope_fd <- function(rf_model, ref_speed, ref_hs, w0, h = 0.2) {
  newd1 <- tibble(Speed_kmh = ref_speed, HillSize_m = ref_hs, WindMS = w0 - h)
  newd2 <- tibble(Speed_kmh = ref_speed, HillSize_m = ref_hs, WindMS = w0 + h)
  y1 <- predict(rf_model, newdata = newd1)
  y2 <- predict(rf_model, newdata = newd2)
  as.numeric((y2 - y1) / (2*h))  # meters per (m/s)
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Metrics for test scoring
# ----------------------------------------------------------
rmse <- function(y, yhat) sqrt(mean((y - yhat)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
r2   <- function(y, yhat) 1 - sum((y - yhat)^2, na.rm = TRUE) / sum((y - mean(y, na.rm = TRUE))^2, na.rm = TRUE)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Estimate model-based wind factor for ONE side and ONE model
#    (NOW drops NAs inside)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
estimate_side_factor <- function(df_train_side, model_type = c("lm","rf")) {
  model_type <- match.arg(model_type)
  req <- c("Distance_m","Speed_kmh","WindMS","HillSize_m","MeterValue_pts_per_m")
  if (!all(req %in% names(df_train_side))) return(NULL)

  # Drop NAs to avoid na.fail in lm/predict
  df_train_side <- df_train_side %>% tidyr::drop_na(dplyr::all_of(req))
  if (nrow(df_train_side) < 20) return(NULL)

  mv <- median(df_train_side$MeterValue_pts_per_m, na.rm = TRUE)

  if (model_type == "lm") {
    m <- lm(Distance_m ~ Speed_kmh + HillSize_m + WindMS, data = df_train_side)
    slope <- unname(coef(m)["WindMS"])  # meters per (m/s)
  } else {
    m <- randomForest(Distance_m ~ Speed_kmh + HillSize_m + WindMS,
                      data = df_train_side, importance = TRUE)
    ref_speed <- median(df_train_side$Speed_kmh,  na.rm = TRUE)
    ref_hs    <- median(df_train_side$HillSize_m, na.rm = TRUE)
    w0        <- median(df_train_side$WindMS,     na.rm = TRUE)
    slope     <- rf_wind_slope_fd(m, ref_speed, ref_hs, w0, h = 0.2)
  }

  list(
    slope_m_per_ms = slope,
    pts_per_ms     = slope * mv,
    meter_value    = mv,
    model          = m
  )
}
```

```{r}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) Evaluate ONE group's data (e.g., a single HillBin or Location)
#    (NOW cleans NAs before split; predicts on complete test rows only)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
evaluate_one_group <- function(df_group, group_label) {
  # Keep only rows with all required fields present
  req <- c("Distance_m","Speed_kmh","WindMS","HillSize_m",
           "MeterValue_pts_per_m","WindPts","HeadWindFactor","TailWindFactor")
  df_group <- df_group %>% tidyr::drop_na(dplyr::all_of(req))

  # 80/20 split at group level
  tt <- train_test_split(df_group, prop = 0.8, seed = 2026)
  train_all <- tt$train
  test_all  <- tt$test

  # Official factors taken from TRAIN (robust to outliers)
  off_head <- suppressWarnings(median(train_all$HeadWindFactor, na.rm = TRUE))
  off_tail <- suppressWarnings(median(train_all$TailWindFactor, na.rm = TRUE))

  # helper to produce rows for a given side
  build_rows_for_side <- function(side_label, official_factor) {
    if (side_label == "head") {
      train_side <- dplyr::filter(train_all, WindMS > 0)
      test_side  <- dplyr::filter(test_all,  WindMS > 0)
    } else {
      train_side <- dplyr::filter(train_all, WindMS < 0)
      test_side  <- dplyr::filter(test_all,  WindMS < 0)
    }

    # Ensure complete cases for TEST before scoring/predicting
    test_req <- c("Distance_m","Speed_kmh","WindMS","HillSize_m","MeterValue_pts_per_m","WindPts")
    test_side_cc <- test_side %>% tidyr::drop_na(dplyr::all_of(test_req))

    out_rows <- list()
    for (mt in c("Linear","RandomForest")) {
      mt_code <- if (mt == "Linear") "lm" else "rf"
      fit <- estimate_side_factor(train_side, mt_code)

      if (is.null(fit)) {
        out_rows[[mt]] <- tibble(
          Group = group_label, side = side_label, Model = mt,
          n_train_side = nrow(train_side), n_test_side = nrow(test_side_cc),
          model_m_per_ms = NA_real_,
          model_pts_per_ms = NA_real_,
          official_pts_per_ms = official_factor,
          gap_pts_per_ms = NA_real_,
          gap_pct = NA_real_,
          RMSE = NA_real_, R2 = NA_real_,
          windpts_bias_mean = NA_real_,
          windpts_mae = NA_real_,
          windpts_iqr = NA_real_
        )
      } else {
        # Distance scoring on TEST (complete cases only)
        if (nrow(test_side_cc) > 0) {
          yhat <- predict(fit$model, newdata = test_side_cc)
          m_rmse <- rmse(test_side_cc$Distance_m, yhat)
          m_r2   <- r2(test_side_cc$Distance_m, yhat)
        } else {
          m_rmse <- NA_real_; m_r2 <- NA_real_
        }

        # WindPts residuals on TEST using this model's slope
        if (nrow(test_side_cc) > 0 && !is.na(fit$slope_m_per_ms)) {
          delta_m_hat <- fit$slope_m_per_ms * test_side_cc$WindMS
          windpts_hat <- - delta_m_hat * test_side_cc$MeterValue_pts_per_m
          resids <- test_side_cc$WindPts - windpts_hat
          bias_mean <- mean(resids, na.rm = TRUE)
          mae       <- mean(abs(resids), na.rm = TRUE)
          iqrv      <- IQR(resids, na.rm = TRUE)
        } else {
          bias_mean <- mae <- iqrv <- NA_real_
        }

        gap_pts <- fit$pts_per_ms - official_factor
        gap_pct <- 100 * gap_pts / official_factor

        out_rows[[mt]] <- tibble(
          Group = group_label, side = side_label, Model = mt,
          n_train_side = nrow(train_side), n_test_side = nrow(test_side_cc),
          model_m_per_ms = fit$slope_m_per_ms,
          model_pts_per_ms = fit$pts_per_ms,
          official_pts_per_ms = official_factor,
          gap_pts_per_ms = gap_pts,
          gap_pct = gap_pct,
          RMSE = m_rmse, R2 = m_r2,
          windpts_bias_mean = bias_mean,
          windpts_mae = mae,
          windpts_iqr = iqrv
        )
      }
    }
    bind_rows(out_rows$Linear, out_rows$RandomForest)
  }

  bind_rows(
    build_rows_for_side("head", off_head),
    build_rows_for_side("tail", off_tail)
  )
}
```

```{r}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5) MAIN: evaluate across groups (split + map) — robust to NAs
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
evaluate_wind_compensation_tt <- function(df, level = c("HillBin","Location")) {
  level <- match.arg(level)
  needed <- c("Distance_m","Speed_kmh","WindMS","HillSize_m",
              "MeterValue_pts_per_m","WindPts","HeadWindFactor","TailWindFactor", level)
  stopifnot(all(needed %in% names(df)))

  # Split df into a named list of data.frames by the grouping column (drop NA groups)
  parts <- split(df, df[[level]], drop = TRUE)

  # Map over groups and bind the results
  res <- purrr::imap(parts, ~ evaluate_one_group(.x, group_label = .y))
  dplyr::bind_rows(res) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(Group = as.character(Group)) %>%
    dplyr::relocate(Group, side, Model)
}
```



```{r}


# ============================================================
# Wind Compensation Evaluation — Sorted by |gap_pct|
# ============================================================

# --- By HillBin ---
results_bin <- evaluate_wind_compensation_tt(df_clean, level = "HillBin") %>%
  # Add absolute gap column
  mutate(abs_gap_pct = abs(gap_pct)) %>%
  # Sort by smallest absolute percent gap
  arrange((abs_gap_pct))

# View results
results_bin


# --- By Location (data-rich venues only) ---
df_loc <- df_clean %>%
  tidyr::drop_na(Distance_m, Speed_kmh, WindMS, HillSize_m, MeterValue_pts_per_m) %>%
  dplyr::add_count(Location, name = "n_loc") %>%
  dplyr::filter(n_loc >= 150)

results_loc <- evaluate_wind_compensation_tt(df_loc, level = "Location") %>%
  # Add absolute gap column
  mutate(abs_gap_pct = abs(gap_pct)) %>%
  # Sort by smallest absolute percent gap
  arrange((abs_gap_pct))

# View results
results_loc

## PLOTTING

# Filter linear only
bin_linear <- results_bin %>%
  filter(Model == "Linear")

p <- ggplot(bin_rf, aes(x = Group, y = mean_abs_gap_pct, fill = side)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(mean_abs_gap_pct, 1), "%")),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
    vjust = -0.3,
    size = 7   # increased for readability
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Wind Direction",
    values = ski_palette,
    labels = c("head" = "Headwind", "tail" = "Tailwind")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 220)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Random Forest",
    subtitle = NULL,
    x        = "Hill size bin",
    y        = "Percent Error"
  ) +
  theme_ski() +
  theme(
    text = element_text(size = 22),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 26),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 26, angle = 90),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 22),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 24),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 32),
    plot.subtitle = element_blank()
  )


#ggsave("female/randfor_gap_perc_hill.png", plot = p, width = 16, height = 12,dpi = 600, type = "cairo-png")


p <- ggplot(bin_linear, aes(x = Group, y = abs_gap_pct, fill = side)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(abs_gap_pct, 1), "%")),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
    vjust = -0.3,
    size = 7   # increased for readability
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Wind Direction",
    values = ski_palette,
    labels = c("head" = "Headwind", "tail" = "Tailwind")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 220)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Linear",
    subtitle = NULL,
    x        = "Hill size bin",
    y        = "Percent Error"
  ) +
  theme_ski() +
  theme(
    text = element_text(size = 22),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 26),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 26, angle = 90),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 22),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 24),
    plot.title = element_text(size =32),
    plot.subtitle = element_blank()
  )



#ggsave("female/linear_gap_perc_hill.png", plot = p, width = 16, height = 12,dpi = 600, type = "cairo-png")



# Identify the top 5 locations by total jumps
top5_locs <- df_loc %>%
  group_by(Location) %>%
  summarise(n = n()) %>%
  arrange(desc(n)) %>%
  slice(1:5) %>%
  pull(Location)

results_loc_top5 <- results_loc %>%
  filter(Group %in% top5_locs)

p <- ggplot(
  results_loc_top5 %>% filter(Model == "Linear"),
  aes(x = Group, y = abs_gap_pct, fill = side)
) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(abs_gap_pct, 1), "%")),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
    vjust = -0.3,
    size = 7   # increased for readability
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Wind Direction",
    values = ski_palette,
    labels = c("head" = "Headwind", "tail" = "Tailwind")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 220)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Linear",
    subtitle = NULL,
    x        = "Venue",
    y        = "Percent Error"
  ) +
  theme_ski() +
  theme(
    text = element_text(size = 22),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 26),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 26, angle = 90),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 22),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 24),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 32),
    plot.subtitle = element_blank()
  )


#ggsave("female/linear_gap_perc_venue.png", plot = p, width = 16, height = 12,dpi = 600, type = "cairo-png")



p <- ggplot(
  results_loc_top5 %>% filter(Model == "RandomForest"),
  aes(x = Group, y = abs_gap_pct, fill = side)
) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(abs_gap_pct, 1), "%")),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
    vjust = -0.3,
    size = 7   # increased for readability
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Wind Direction",
    values = ski_palette,
    labels = c("head" = "Headwind", "tail" = "Tailwind")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 220)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Random Forest",
    subtitle = NULL,
    x        = "Venue",
    y        = "Percent Error"
  ) +
  theme_ski() +
  theme(
    text = element_text(size = 22),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 26),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 26, angle = 90),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 22),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 24),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 32),
    plot.subtitle = element_blank()
  )


#ggsave("female/randfor_gap_perc_venue.png",plot  = p,width = 16,height = 12,type  = "cairo-png",dpi   = 600)





```




```{r}


# ============================================================
# Wind Compensation Evaluation — Sorted by |gap_pct|
# ============================================================

# --- By HillBin ---
results_bin <- evaluate_wind_compensation_tt(df_clean, level = "HillBin") %>%
  # Add absolute gap column
  mutate(abs_gap_pct = abs(gap_pct)) %>%
  # Sort by smallest absolute percent gap
  arrange((abs_gap_pct))

# View results
results_bin


# --- By Location (data-rich venues only) ---
df_loc <- df_clean %>%
  tidyr::drop_na(Distance_m, Speed_kmh, WindMS, HillSize_m, MeterValue_pts_per_m) %>%
  dplyr::add_count(Location, name = "n_loc") %>%
  dplyr::filter(n_loc >= 150)

results_loc <- evaluate_wind_compensation_tt(df_loc, level = "Location") %>%
  # Add absolute gap column
  mutate(abs_gap_pct = abs(gap_pct)) %>%
  # Sort by smallest absolute percent gap
  arrange((abs_gap_pct))

# View results
results_loc

## PLOTTING

# Filter linear only
bin_linear <- results_bin %>%
  filter(Model == "Linear")


bin_rf <- results_bin %>%
  filter(Model == "RandomForest") %>%
  group_by(Group, side) %>%
  summarise(mean_abs_gap_pct = mean(abs_gap_pct, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

p <- ggplot(bin_rf, aes(x = Group, y = mean_abs_gap_pct, fill = side)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(mean_abs_gap_pct, 1), "%")),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
    vjust = -0.3,
    size = 7   # increased for readability
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Wind Direction",
    values = ski_palette,
    labels = c("head" = "Headwind", "tail" = "Tailwind")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 220)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Random Forest",
    subtitle = NULL,
    x        = "Hill size bin",
    y        = "Percent Error"
  ) +
  theme_ski() +
  theme(
    text = element_text(size = 22),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 26),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 26, angle = 90),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 22),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 24),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 32),
    plot.subtitle = element_blank()
  )


#ggsave("female/randfor_gap_perc_hill.png", plot = p, width = 16, height = 12,dpi = 600, type = "cairo-png")


p <- ggplot(bin_linear, aes(x = Group, y = abs_gap_pct, fill = side)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(abs_gap_pct, 1), "%")),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
    vjust = -0.3,
    size = 7   # upgraded from 4 for readability
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Wind Direction",
    values = ski_palette,
    labels = c("head" = "Headwind", "tail" = "Tailwind")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 220)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Linear",
    subtitle = NULL,
    x        = "Hill size bin",
    y        = "Percent Error"
  ) +
  theme_ski() +
  theme(
    text = element_text(size = 22),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 26),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 26, angle = 90),  # vertical y-label
    axis.text = element_text(size = 22),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 24),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 32),
    plot.subtitle = element_blank()
  )


#ggsave("female/linear_gap_perc_hill.png", plot = p, width = 16, height = 12,dpi = 600, type = "cairo-png")



# Identify the top 5 locations by total jumps
top5_locs <- df_loc %>%
  group_by(Location) %>%
  summarise(n = n()) %>%
  arrange(desc(n)) %>%
  slice(1:5) %>%
  pull(Location)

results_loc_top5 <- results_loc %>%
  filter(Group %in% top5_locs)

p <- ggplot(
  results_loc_top5 %>% filter(Model == "Linear"),
  aes(x = Group, y = abs_gap_pct, fill = side)
) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(abs_gap_pct, 1), "%")),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
    vjust = -0.3,
    size = 7    # increased for readability
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Wind Direction",
    values = ski_palette,
    labels = c("head" = "Headwind", "tail" = "Tailwind")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 220)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Linear",
    subtitle = NULL,
    x        = "Venue",
    y        = "Percent Error"
  ) +
  theme_ski() +
  theme(
    text = element_text(size = 22),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 26),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 26, angle = 90),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 22),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 24),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 32),
    plot.subtitle = element_blank()
  )


#ggsave("female/linear_gap_perc_venue.png", plot = p, width = 16, height = 12,dpi = 600, type = "cairo-png")



p <- ggplot(
  results_loc_top5 %>% filter(Model == "RandomForest"),
  aes(x = Group, y = abs_gap_pct, fill = side)
) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(abs_gap_pct, 1), "%")),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
    vjust = -0.3,
    size = 7    # increased from 4 for readability
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Wind Direction",
    values = ski_palette,
    labels = c("head" = "Headwind", "tail" = "Tailwind")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 220)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Random Forest",
    subtitle = NULL,
    x        = "Venue",
    y        = "Percent Error"
  ) +
  theme_ski() +
  theme(
    text = element_text(size = 22),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 26),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 26, angle = 90),  # vertical orientation
    axis.text = element_text(size = 22),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 24),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 32),
    plot.subtitle = element_blank()
  )


#ggsave("female/randfor_gap_perc_venue.png",plot  = p,width = 16,height = 12,type  = "cairo-png",dpi   = 600)


```




```{r}
## ---- Prepare data (make one row per venue–side–model, with ordering) ----

# Make sure results_loc_top10 already exists as your filtered-by-top-10-side table.
# If not, you can recreate it like this (safe to re-run):

## ---- Build Top-10 Venue–Side Table (Exactly 10 Rows) ----

library(forcats)

# Top 10 venue–side combos for the Linear model
lin_data <- results_loc %>%
  filter(Model == "Linear") %>%
  mutate(
    n_side    = n_train_side + n_test_side,
    GroupSide = fct_reorder(paste(Group, side, sep = "___"), n_side, .desc = TRUE),
    GroupSide = fct_rev(GroupSide)  # forces largest to appear at the top after coord_flip
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(n_side)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)


# Top 10 venue–side combos for the Random Forest model
rf_data <- results_loc %>%
  filter(Model == "RandomForest") %>%
  mutate(
    n_side    = n_train_side + n_test_side,
    GroupSide = fct_reorder(paste(Group, side, sep = "___"), n_side, .desc = TRUE),
    GroupSide = fct_rev(GroupSide)  # same fix
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(n_side)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)



## ---- Linear ----
p <- ggplot(
  lin_data,
  aes(x = GroupSide, y = abs_gap_pct, fill = side)
) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(abs_gap_pct, 1), "%")),
    hjust = -0.2,
    size  = 7   # was 3; much easier to read
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    labels = function(x) sub("___.*$", "", x)   # show only venue name on axis
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name   = "Wind direction",
    values = ski_palette[c("head", "tail")],
    labels = c(head = "Headwind", tail = "Tailwind")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 125),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))
  ) +
  coord_flip(clip = "off") +
  labs(
    title    = "Linear - Female",
    subtitle = NULL,
    x        = "Venue",
    y        = "Percent error"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
  text = element_text(size = 30),

  axis.title.x = element_text(size = 36),
  axis.title.y = element_text(size = 36, angle = 90),

  axis.text.x = element_text(size = 30),
  axis.text.y = element_text(size = 30),

  legend.title = element_text(size = 32),
  legend.text  = element_text(size = 28),

  plot.title = element_text(size = 44),
  plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 34),

  strip.text = element_text(size = 32),   # if faceting

  panel.grid       = element_blank(),
  panel.border     = element_blank(),
  panel.background = element_blank()
)


p

#ggsave( "female/linear_gap_perc_venue_top10_side.png",plot   = p,width  = 12,height = 8,dpi    = 600,type   = "cairo-png")


## ---- Random Forest ----
p <- ggplot(
  rf_data,
  aes(x = GroupSide, y = abs_gap_pct, fill = side)
) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(abs_gap_pct, 1), "%")),
    hjust = -0.2,
    size  = 7    # increased from 3 to match other figures
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    labels = function(x) sub("___.*$", "", x)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name   = "Wind direction",
    values = ski_palette[c("head", "tail")],
    labels = c(head = "Headwind", tail = "Tailwind")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 125),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))
  ) +
  coord_flip(clip = "off") +
  labs(
    title    = "Random Forest - Female",
    subtitle = NULL,
    x        = "Venue",
    y        = "Percent error"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
  text = element_text(size = 30),

  axis.title.x = element_text(size = 36),
  axis.title.y = element_text(size = 36, angle = 90),

  axis.text.x = element_text(size = 30),
  axis.text.y = element_text(size = 30),

  legend.title = element_text(size = 32),
  legend.text  = element_text(size = 28),

  plot.title = element_text(size = 44),
  plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 34),

  strip.text = element_text(size = 32),   # if faceting

  panel.grid       = element_blank(),
  panel.border     = element_blank(),
  panel.background = element_blank()
)

p

#ggsave("female/randfor_gap_perc_venue_top10_side.png",plot   = p,width  = 12,height = 8,dpi    = 600,type   = "cairo-png")


  
```


```{r}

# ---- 8) plotting helper (quick comparison) ----
# Scatter of model-based vs official factors (points/ms) with 45° line.
plot_compensation_compare <- function(results_tbl, title = "Model vs Official Wind Factors (points per m/s)") {
  req <- c("Group","side","model_pts_per_ms","official_pts_per_ms")
  stopifnot(all(req %in% names(results_tbl)))

  ggplot(results_tbl, aes(x = official_pts_per_ms, y = model_pts_per_ms, label = Group)) +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = 2) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.75) +
    ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ side) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      x = "Official factor (points per m/s)",
      y = "Model-based factor (points per m/s)",
      title = title
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
}

# Examples:
plot_compensation_compare(results_bin, "Bins: Model vs Official")
plot_compensation_compare(results_loc, "Venues: Model vs Official")


```


```{r}

# ---- 8) plotting helper (quick comparison) ----
# Scatter of model-based vs official factors (points/ms) with 45° line.
plot_compensation_compare <- function(results_tbl, title = "Model vs Official Wind Factors (points per m/s)") {
  req <- c("Group","side","model_pts_per_ms","official_pts_per_ms")
  stopifnot(all(req %in% names(results_tbl)))

  ggplot(results_tbl, aes(x = official_pts_per_ms, y = model_pts_per_ms, label = Group)) +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = 2) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.75) +
    ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ side) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      x = "Official factor (points per m/s)",
      y = "Model-based factor (points per m/s)",
      title = title
    ) + scale_fill_ski()
}

# Examples:
plot_compensation_compare(results_bin, "Bins: Model vs Official")
plot_compensation_compare(results_loc, "Venues: Model vs Official")


```
